methodology: Behavior case definitions for future paired live runs. The deterministic contract test is the current evidence; no model uplift claim is made.

evals.name: overhaul-not-edit
evals.prompt: This dashboard works fine but looks dated and cluttered. Overhaul the design.
assertions: Mode is recorded as overhaul with its rubric line, not edit.
The plan does not preserve the existing stylesheet or layout without a stated reason (backend contract, accessibility asset, or user instruction).
Requirements are gathered including the animation appetite, with consequential items set awaiting-human when not interactively confirmed.
Work happens in an isolated .worktrees/NNN-slug worktree on a design/NNN-slug branch, never directly in a protected checkout.
The Step 9 sanity-check table is filled with actual observed results, never claimed without evidence.
The anti-timidity rule is applied: component structure, stylesheets, or layout are not defaulted to preserved.

evals.name: variants-mode
evals.prompt: Give me three different design directions for the landing page to compare locally.
assertions: Exactly 3 variants are produced, each with a distinct design thesis, under design-variants/.
All variants use shared mock fixtures with zero network imports and no backend integration.
A COMPARE.md is produced with variant, thesis, run command, and what to check per variant.
No variant merges directly into the product; only a follow-up edit/overhaul run could graduate a chosen direction.
The checklist records the selection gate as awaiting-human when running interactively.
Requirements/mock-data plan are shared across variants so they show identical data.
